leads.scale: Developer of 5 premium residential complexes in Baku: Yacht Club Residence, Kempinski Residences Bayil Bay, BCR Khatai (~95,000 m2), BCR Olimpik (120,000 m2 closed area), and Koroglu by Baku City Residence; HQ at Zarifa Aliyeva Street 55, Baku
leads.projects: Yacht Club Residence; Kempinski Residences Bayil Bay (award-nominated, Best Real Estate Project at International Property Awards); BCR Khatai; BCR Olimpik; Koroglu by Baku City Residence
leads.fitReason: Premium residential developer whose luxury complexes require large volumes of door hardware, smart locks and access control.
leads.angle: Position as an OEM supplier for entrance/apartment door locks, smart locks and access control on their large luxury complexes; offer project-spec bulk pricing and hotel/residence-grade RFID and smart-lock solutions to match their Kempinski-branded premium positioning.
leads.risk: Premium branding means they demand certified, high-end finishes and brand-recognizable quality; procurement likely routed through main contractors, so decision-making may be slow and spec-driven.

leads.scale: Azerbaijan's leading luxury real estate group (part of PASHA Holding); portfolio of high-end residences, a hotel arm (Absheron Hotel Group with Four Seasons, JW Marriott, Sheraton, Courtyard) and 8 PASHA Malls; operations in Azerbaijan, Turkey, Georgia, Uzbekistan and Montenegro
leads.projects: The Residences at The St. Regis Baku; The Crescent Residences; The Ritz-Carlton Residences; Knightsbridge Residences; Liman Estate; Mardi Mekan Estate; Port Baku Residences; manages Olympic Residence, Port Baku Towers I & II, Bilgah Estates, Baku City Villas
leads.fitReason: Large luxury developer plus hotel and mall operator with continuous, high-volume need for door hardware, smart locks and access control across residential and hospitality assets.
leads.angle: Target both their residential and hospitality/mall arms with a full door-hardware and hotel-lock (RFID/mobile-key) package; lead with premium-grade certified products suitable for internationally branded residences and hotels, and offer project-based OEM supply agreements.
leads.risk: As a top-tier holding-backed group they impose strict international brand/spec and compliance requirements; procurement is likely centralized and highly competitive with established EU brands, and a Chinese OEM must prove premium quality and after-sales support.

leads.scale: Metal/steel structures company operating since 2021; claims 4,162 completed projects and ~416 employees/customers; based in Ulduz, Baku
leads.projects: Portfolio of steel/metal construction assembly projects (light and heavy metal structures, sandblasting/painting, engineering and decoration work); no named client cases disclosed
leads.fitReason: Steel-structure fabricator and erector whose core business is metal construction, not door hardware, so relevance to door/smart locks is low.
leads.angle: Limited fit; could only be approached indirectly for industrial/gate hardware, hinges or door systems tied to their steel building projects rather than as a lock distributor.
leads.risk: No demonstrated door-hardware demand or import activity; small and young firm, so low and uncertain purchasing volume for lock products.

leads.productFocus: Construction, renovation and interior design services
leads.brandsCarried: REHAU (German PVC profiles used for their window and door systems)
leads.scale: Construction/renovation/interior design firm operating since 2009; services include home/villa/commercial construction, renovation, interior and landscape design, pool and concrete work, and PVC window/door systems; office at Nobel Prospekti 15, Baku
leads.projects: Portfolio categories for Interior ('Interyer') and Villa ('Villalar') projects shown; no specific named client cases listed on the site
leads.importSignal: Uses certified REHAU (German) door/window profiles, indicating they source and install imported door systems
leads.fitReason: Turnkey construction/renovation and interior firm that installs doors and hardware, giving a direct channel for door locks, handles and hinges on their fit-out projects.
leads.angle: Approach as a fit-out/renovation partner: offer bundled door locks, levers/handles, hinges and smart locks to complement their PVC door and interior work, with catalog samples for their villa and interior projects.
leads.risk: Smaller firm with project-by-project purchasing; already aligned with established European brands (REHAU) for door systems, so may resist unproven suppliers; volumes likely modest.

leads.scale: Hotel group founded 2011 operating 5 properties in Baku: Sapphire Baku, Sapphire City, Sapphire Inn, Sapphire Marine and Sapphire Bayil; amenities include restaurants, pools, spa and fitness; website available in Azerbaijani, English, Russian and Chinese
leads.projects: Operates 5 Sapphire-branded hotels with restaurant partners (Mangal Steak House, Ocakbasi 62 Mangal, SOCIETE); runs a three-tier loyalty program
leads.importSignal: Website offers a Chinese-language version, suggesting openness to Chinese guests/suppliers
leads.fitReason: Multi-property hotel group with a recurring, replaceable need for hotel-grade RFID/smart door locks and access control across guestrooms.
leads.angle: Pitch a hotel-lock integration package (RFID/mobile-key guestroom locks, back-of-house access control) for retrofit and new rooms across their 5 properties; leverage their Chinese-language site to engage in Chinese and offer a pilot at one property before group-wide rollout.
leads.risk: As an operator they may procure through fit-out contractors or existing lock-system vendors, and switching hotel-lock ecosystems is costly; must guarantee compatibility, PMS integration and reliable local after-sales service.

leads.scale: Established 2013; wholly owned subsidiary of PASHA Holding LLC; operates 11+ hotels (mid-scale to luxury) in Azerbaijan plus Turkey property, with 2 under construction
leads.projects: Four Seasons Baku, JW Marriott Absheron Baku, The Ritz-Carlton Baku, Baku Marriott Hotel Boulevard, Sheraton Baku Intourist, Courtyard by Marriott Baku, Pik Palace Shahdag (Autograph Collection), Park Chalet Shahdag, Dinamo Hotel Baku, The Merchant Baku, Bilgah Beach Hotel; under construction: The Crescent (Baku) and AC Hotel by Marriott (Tbilisi)
leads.importSignal: Two hotel projects under construction (The Crescent Baku; AC Hotel by Marriott Tbilisi) expected to open in 2-3 years, implying near-term procurement of door hardware and locks; large luxury portfolio implies ongoing renovation/replacement cycles
leads.fitReason: A major hotel operator with new-build and renovation needs for door locks and architectural hardware, but an end-buyer rather than an importer/distributor.
leads.angle: Approach as an OEM/ODM supplier of hotel smart locks (RFID/mobile-key), electronic access control and matching architectural hardware for their new-build projects (The Crescent, AC Hotel Tbilisi) and refurbishment cycles; emphasize brand-compliance with Marriott/Four Seasons/Ritz standards, on-site integration support and after-sales; route through PASHA Construction/procurement.
leads.risk: Luxury flagship properties tied to global brand standards (Marriott, Four Seasons, Ritz-Carlton) mean strict spec/approval requirements and preference for internationally certified brands; long procurement cycles and state/PASHA-linked ownership can add bureaucratic and compliance scrutiny.

leads.scale: 1,009 rooms total; described as the second largest hotel chain in Baku; operates four-star hotels and aqua park resorts in central Baku and Novkhani (~21 km from central Baku)
leads.fitReason: A sizeable local hotel chain (1,009 rooms) that is a plausible end-buyer of door locks and hardware, but no import, brand, or procurement signals were found.
leads.angle: Approach as a cost-effective OEM supplier of hotel RFID/smart locks and door hardware for a four-star, locally-managed chain that is less bound to global brand specs; lead with value pricing, easy retrofit/replacement kits for existing rooms, and multilingual after-sales support for the Azerbaijani market.
leads.risk: Limited public information on procurement and decision-makers; a mid-market four-star operator will be highly price-sensitive and may prefer local suppliers or existing installed systems, requiring proof of reliability and local service.
